title: oxalis subintegrifolia
intro: Oxalis subintegrifolia, also known as the Mountain Wood-sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the wood sorrel family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and to lower blood pressure, making it useful in the treatment of urinary tract issues and hypertension. The plant's leaves and stems contain anthraquinones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing a range of health benefits including improved digestion and reduced oxidative stress. Oxalis subintegrifolia can be prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, or as an ingredient in herbal supplements and remedies.
